LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF GIOVANNI ZOCCA'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES INCURRED ON APPEAL; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. AS THE COURT OF APPEAL OPINION EARLIER FILED IN THIS CASE NOTES, THE MATTER CONCERNS A TWENTY-FIVE YEAR OLD INTER-FAMILY DISPUTE. WHAT APPARENTLY STARTED AS A $25,000 CASE (REDUCED BY SET-OFF TO NOTHING) HAS SURVIVED OVER THE LAST YEARS JUST AS PROLONGED DISPUTE OVER ATTORNEYS FEES-- FEES WHICH NOW FAR, FAR OUTWEIGH THE WORTH OF THE UNDERLYING CLAIMS. NOW, AS BEFORE, DEFENDANTS' ACTIONS HAVE UNNECESSARILY INCREASED THE COSTS TO PLAINTIFF, NOT TO SPEAK OF TO DEFENDANTS THEMSELVES. HERE, DEFENDANTS PRESENT NO EVIDENCE THAT THE BILLED TIME WAS EXCESSIVE, AND THEY NOTE NO ENTRIES OR TASKS FOR WHICH THE TIME OUGHT NOT TO HAVE SPENT. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE CLAIMED RATE IS EXCESSIVE, AND THE LEGAL ARGUMENT ON THE UNAVAILABILITY OF FEES IS NOT PERSUASIVE: THE APPEAL FOR WHICH FEES ARE SOUGHT INVOLVED ENTITLEMENT TO FEES, WHICH ENTITLEMENT JUSTIFIES THE AWARD NOW. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS UNOPPOSED AND GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR # 7489

Notice Of Hearing Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees Incurred On Appeal; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jul-25-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christopher Cole, Esq. of the Law Office of Christopher Cole, Tel. 415.978.9999;
Bill Hop, Esq. specially appearing for the defendant (no card)

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF GIOVANNI ZOCCA's Motion For Attorneys Fees Incurred On Appeal; Request For Judicial Notice is Granted. As the Court of Appeal opinion earlier filed in this case notes, the matter concerns a twenty-five year old inter-family dispute. What apparently started as a $25,000 case (reduced by set-off to nothing) has survived over the last years just as prolonged dispute over attorneys fees-- fees which now far, far outweigh the worth of the underlying claims. Now, as before, defendants' actions have unnecessarily increased the costs to plaintiff, not to speak of to defendants themselves. Here, defendants present no evidence that the billed time was excessive, and they note no entries or tasks for which the time ought not to have spent. There is no evidence that the claimed rate is excessive, and the legal argument on the unavailability of fees is not persuasive: The appeal for which fees are sought involved entitlement to fees, which entitlement justifies the award now. The request for judicial notice is unopposed and granted. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489
